trans-unit.source: Exercise 3: Trigger & Intervention Map for Forecast-based-Action
trans-unit.target: Exercise 3: Trigger & Intervention Map for Forecast-based-Action

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise is based on the monitoring and triggering process used by the Somalia Red Crescent Society (SRCS) in the framework of a drought Early Action Protocol (EAP).
trans-unit.target: This exercise is based on the monitoring and triggering process used by the Somalia Red Crescent Society (SRCS) in the framework of a drought Early Action Protocol (EAP).

trans-unit.source: Within this exercise, you will build a simplified version of the monitoring and trigger mechanism for the FEWSNET projection pillar.
trans-unit.target: Within this exercise, you will build a simplified version of the monitoring and trigger mechanism for the FEWSNET projection pillar.

trans-unit.source: __Type of trainings exercise:__
trans-unit.target: __Type of trainings exercise:__

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Geodata Import in QGIS](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)
trans-unit.target: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)

trans-unit.source: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Join Attributes by location (summary](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)
trans-unit.target: [Join Attributes by location (summary](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)

trans-unit.source: [Table functions](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing)
trans-unit.target: [Table functions](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing)

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training facilitation.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.md) for some general tips on training facilitation.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Background
trans-unit.target: Background

trans-unit.source: Setting triggers is one of the cornerstones of the __Forecast-based Financing (FbF) system__. For a National Society to have access to automatically released funding for their early actions, their Early Action Protocol needs to clearly define where and when funds will be allocated, and assistance will be provided. In FbF, this is decided according to specific threshold values, so-called __triggers__, based on weather and climate forecasts, which are defined for each region (see [FbF Manual](https://manual.forecast-based-financing.org/en/chapter/set-the-trigger/)).
trans-unit.target: Setting triggers is one of the cornerstones of the __Forecast-based Financing (FbF) system__. For a National Society to have access to automatically released funding for their early actions, their Early Action Protocol needs to clearly define where and when funds will be allocated, and assistance will be provided. In FbF, this is decided according to specific threshold values, so-called __triggers__, based on weather and climate forecasts, which are defined for each region (see [FbF Manual](https://manual.forecast-based-financing.org/en/chapter/set-the-trigger/)).

trans-unit.source: For the development of the Somaliland-Somalia Drought Trigger mechanism, various datasources were thoroughly analysed. Finally, the main parameters chosen for the trigger based on the __historical impact assessment__ are the twelve month Standard Precipitation Index (SPI12) and the IPC acute food insecurity classification. The exact data used are the documented and forecasted SPI12 (source: ICPAC) and the forecasted IPC classification (8 month forecast, source: FEWSNET), that is used to calculate a population weighted index of food insecurity. The trigger thresholds for both components were optimised towards the most favourable proportion of hit rate and false alarm rate. The emerging thresholds were <-1 for the SPI12 and >=0,7 for the IPC based index. The triggering is done on district level and per district just one trigger initiation per year is possible.
trans-unit.target: For the development of the Somaliland-Somalia Drought Trigger mechanism, various datasources were thoroughly analysed. Finally, the main parameters chosen for the trigger based on the __historical impact assessment__ are the twelve month Standard Precipitation Index (SPI12) and the IPC acute food insecurity classification. The exact data used are the documented and forecasted SPI12 (source: ICPAC) and the forecasted IPC classification (8 month forecast, source: FEWSNET), that is used to calculate a population weighted index of food insecurity. The trigger thresholds for both components were optimised towards the most favourable proportion of hit rate and false alarm rate. The emerging thresholds were <-1 for the SPI12 and >=0,7 for the IPC based index. The triggering is done on district level and per district just one trigger initiation per year is possible.

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} Trigger Statement When ICPAC issues a SPI-12 forecast of less than -1 for a district AND the current FEWSNET food insecurity projection reaches at least 0.7 in its derived population weighted index in the same district, then we will act in this district. We expect the lead-time to be 90 days. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} Trigger Statement When ICPAC issues a SPI-12 forecast of less than -1 for a district AND the current FEWSNET food insecurity projection reaches at least 0.7 in its derived population weighted index in the same district, then we will act in this district. We expect the lead-time to be 90 days. :::

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: Food Insecurity Projection data
trans-unit.target: Food Insecurity Projection data

trans-unit.source: The drought trigger mechanism is based on two variable monitoring datasets. One of them Food Insecurity projection produced by FEWSNET which is updated ruffly every month.
trans-unit.target: The drought trigger mechanism is based on two variable monitoring datasets. One of them Food Insecurity projection produced by FEWSNET which is updated ruffly every month.

trans-unit.source: Dataset
trans-unit.target: Dataset

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: IPC Projections
trans-unit.target: IPC Projections

trans-unit.source: [FEWSNET](https://fews.net/)
trans-unit.target: [FEWSNET](https://fews.net/)

trans-unit.source: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.
trans-unit.target: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.

trans-unit.source: What is IPC Food Security Projection Data?
trans-unit.target: What is IPC Food Security Projection Data?

trans-unit.source: The IPC is a commonly accepted measure and classification to describe the current and anticipated severity of acute food insecurity. The classification is based on a convergence of available data and evidence, including indicators related to food consumption, livelihoods, malnutrition and mortality. Food Insecurity is one of the prioritised impacts of droughts in Somalia which is why it is also used for the triggering mechanism, in a population-weighted index.
trans-unit.target: The IPC is a commonly accepted measure and classification to describe the current and anticipated severity of acute food insecurity. The classification is based on a convergence of available data and evidence, including indicators related to food consumption, livelihoods, malnutrition and mortality. Food Insecurity is one of the prioritised impacts of droughts in Somalia which is why it is also used for the triggering mechanism, in a population-weighted index.

trans-unit.source: Three times a year (February, June, and October) FEWSNET estimates most likely IPC classes for the upcoming 8 month (near-term and mid-term projection), available from 2019-current. The near-term projection is called ML1 and is a projection for the upcoming 4 month, the mid-term projection is called ML2 and projects the IPC classes for the 4 subsequent months. For the triggering ML1 (near-term) as well as ML2 (mid-term) projections will be considered.
trans-unit.target: Three times a year (February, June, and October) FEWSNET estimates most likely IPC classes for the upcoming 8 month (near-term and mid-term projection), available from 2019-current. The near-term projection is called ML1 and is a projection for the upcoming 4 month, the mid-term projection is called ML2 and projects the IPC classes for the 4 subsequent months. For the triggering ML1 (near-term) as well as ML2 (mid-term) projections will be considered.

trans-unit.source: Outlook updates are produced almost every month and are also taken into account.
trans-unit.target: Outlook updates are produced almost every month and are also taken into account.

trans-unit.source: Colour
trans-unit.target: Colour

trans-unit.source: Phase
trans-unit.target: Phase

trans-unit.source: Descriptions
trans-unit.target: Descriptions

trans-unit.source: 1. Minimal
trans-unit.target: 1. Minimal

trans-unit.source: Households are able to meet essential food and non-food needs without engaging in atypical and unsustainable strategies to access food and income.
trans-unit.target: Households are able to meet essential food and non-food needs without engaging in atypical and unsustainable strategies to access food and income.

trans-unit.source: 2. Stressed
trans-unit.target: 2. Stressed

trans-unit.source: Households have minimally adequate food consumption but are unable to afford some essential non-food expenditures without engaging in stress-coping strategies.
trans-unit.target: Households have minimally adequate food consumption but are unable to afford some essential non-food expenditures without engaging in stress-coping strategies.

trans-unit.source: 3. Crisis
trans-unit.target: 3. Crisis

trans-unit.source: Households either have food consumption gaps that are reflected by high or above-usual acute malnutrition __OR__ are marginally able to meet minimum food needs but only by depleting essential livelihood assets or through crisis-coping strategies.
trans-unit.target: Households either have food consumption gaps that are reflected by high or above-usual acute malnutrition __OR__ are marginally able to meet minimum food needs but only by depleting essential livelihood assets or through crisis-coping strategies.

trans-unit.source: 4. Emergency
trans-unit.target: 4. Emergency

trans-unit.source: Households either have large food consumption gaps which are reflected in very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; __OR__ are able to mitigate large food consumption gaps but only by employing emergency livelihood strategies and asset liquidation.
trans-unit.target: Households either have large food consumption gaps which are reflected in very high acute malnutrition and excess mortality; __OR__ are able to mitigate large food consumption gaps but only by employing emergency livelihood strategies and asset liquidation.

trans-unit.source: 5. Famine
trans-unit.target: 5. Famine

trans-unit.source: Households have an extreme lack of food and/or other basic needs even after full employment of coping strategies. Starvation, death, destitution, and extremely critical acute malnutrition levels are evident. (For Famine Classification, area needs to have extreme critical levels of acute malnutrition and mortality.)
trans-unit.target: Households have an extreme lack of food and/or other basic needs even after full employment of coping strategies. Starvation, death, destitution, and extremely critical acute malnutrition levels are evident. (For Famine Classification, area needs to have extreme critical levels of acute malnutrition and mortality.)

trans-unit.source: Training Data
trans-unit.target: Training Data

trans-unit.source: Download the data folder __[here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Modul_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger.zip)__ and save it on your PC. Unzip the .zip file!
trans-unit.target: Download the data folder __[here](https://nexus.heigit.org/repository/gis-training-resource-center/Modul_5/Modul_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Modul_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger.zip)__ and save it on your PC. Unzip the .zip file!

trans-unit.source: For this particular exercise, we will use a combination of pre-processed data and the download of real data from [FEWS.net](https://fews.net/). The preprocessed datasets are:
trans-unit.target: For this particular exercise, we will use a combination of pre-processed data and the download of real data from [FEWS.net](https://fews.net/). The preprocessed datasets are:

trans-unit.source: Dataset
trans-unit.target: Dataset

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: Administrative boundaries
trans-unit.target: Administrative boundaries

trans-unit.source: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-som?)
trans-unit.target: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-som?)

trans-unit.source: The administrative boundaries on level 0-2 for Somalia and Somaliland can be accessed via HDX. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 2 (district level) as a shapefile. We have added the population number for each district derived from Worldpop.
trans-unit.target: The administrative boundaries on level 0-2 for Somalia and Somaliland can be accessed via HDX. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 2 (district level) as a shapefile. We have added the population number for each district derived from Worldpop.

trans-unit.source: Population Counts
trans-unit.target: Population Counts

trans-unit.source: [Worldpop](https://hub.worldpop.org/doi/10.5258/SOTON/WP00534)
trans-unit.target: [Worldpop](https://hub.worldpop.org/doi/10.5258/SOTON/WP00534)

trans-unit.source: The worldpop dataset in .geotif raster format provides population estimates per hectare for the year 2020
trans-unit.target: The worldpop dataset in .geotif raster format provides population estimates per hectare for the year 2020

trans-unit.source: Whereas the IPC-Projections data will be downloaded by the participants directly from FEWS.net.
trans-unit.target: Whereas the IPC-Projections data will be downloaded by the participants directly from FEWS.net.

trans-unit.source: Dataset
trans-unit.target: Dataset

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Description
trans-unit.target: Description

trans-unit.source: IPC Projections
trans-unit.target: IPC Projections

trans-unit.source: [FEWSNET](https://fews.net/)
trans-unit.target: [FEWSNET](https://fews.net/)

trans-unit.source: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.
trans-unit.target: five-phase scale providing common standards for classifying the severity of acute or anticipated acute food insecurity.

trans-unit.source: Task
trans-unit.target: Task

trans-unit.source: Step 1: Setting up folder structure
trans-unit.target: Step 1: Setting up folder structure

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consistent results.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, we set up the correct folder structure to make the analysis easier and to ensure consistent results.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed.
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No special tools or programs are needed.

trans-unit.source: Instruction
trans-unit.target: Instruction

trans-unit.source: Folder Structure
trans-unit.target: Folder Structure

trans-unit.source: Open the Folder “Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger"
trans-unit.target: Open the Folder “Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger"

trans-unit.source: Open the subfolder "Monitoring"
trans-unit.target: Open the subfolder "Monitoring"

trans-unit.source: Copy the Template folder "TEMPLATE_Year_Month" and change the name to the current year and month `2024_01`.
trans-unit.target: Copy the Template folder "TEMPLATE_Year_Month" and change the name to the current year and month `2024_01`.

trans-unit.source: The Video below shows the process for setting up the folder for December 2023.
trans-unit.target: The Video below shows the process for setting up the folder for December 2023.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up folder structure
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Setting up folder structure
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 2: Download of the forecast data
trans-unit.target: Step 2: Download of the forecast data

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, we will download the forecast data on which our triggers will be based.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, we will download the forecast data on which our triggers will be based.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ Internet Browser
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ Internet Browser

trans-unit.source: The IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications, as well as the updates of the IPC data, amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.
trans-unit.target: The IPC data will be pulled from the FEWSNET website. FEWS NET publishes IPC data on its website. The main data publications, as well as the updates of the IPC data, amount to the publication of new data almost monthly.

trans-unit.source: IPC Data
trans-unit.target: IPC Data

trans-unit.source: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [FEWSNET Website](https://fews.net/). On the website, you will have to click on Somalia to access the data. Alternatively, you can navigate through `Data` → `Acute Food Insecurity Data` and enter "Somalia". In the menu you will see different data formats for different timestamps. Once you find out which timestamp is the most current one, find the ZIP download. We need the data in shapefile (.shp) format, which is only included in the ZIP file and not provided as single download file.
trans-unit.target: The IPC Projection data is provided and regularly updated on the [FEWSNET Website](https://fews.net/). On the website, you will have to click on Somalia to access the data. Alternatively, you can navigate through `Data` → `Acute Food Insecurity Data` and enter "Somalia". In the menu you will see different data formats for different timestamps. Once you find out which timestamp is the most current one, find the ZIP download. We need the data in shapefile (.shp) format, which is only included in the ZIP file and not provided as single download file.

trans-unit.source: Go to [FEWSNET Website](https://fews.net/). Click on `Data` → `Acute Food Insecurity`.
trans-unit.target: Go to [FEWSNET Website](https://fews.net/). Click on `Data` → `Acute Food Insecurity`.

trans-unit.source: Scroll down. In `Geographic Area`, type in “Somalia” and click `Apply`.
trans-unit.target: Scroll down. In `Geographic Area`, type in “Somalia” and click `Apply`.

trans-unit.source: Choose the newest dataset.
trans-unit.target: Choose the newest dataset.

source.ph: name: FEWSNET IPC - Download IPC Projections

target.ph: name: FEWSNET IPC - Download IPC Projections

trans-unit.source: Download the one with the __ZIP__ Data.
trans-unit.target: Download the one with the __ZIP__ Data.

trans-unit.source: When you have downloaded the data, right-click on the file and click on `Extract all` → `Extract`.
trans-unit.target: When you have downloaded the data, right-click on the file and click on `Extract all` → `Extract`.

trans-unit.source: Open the extracted folder and copy the ML1 data in the IPC_ML1 folder you have created in step 1.
trans-unit.target: Open the extracted folder and copy the ML1 data in the IPC_ML1 folder you have created in step 1.

trans-unit.source: The filename is composed of "SO" for Somalia, year and month of the report month e.g `SO_202308_ML1.shp`. Example path: `.../Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/IPC_ML1`. :::{Warning} Make sure to __not__ use the ML1_IDP data which comes in the .zip folder as well! :::
trans-unit.target: The filename is composed of "SO" for Somalia, year and month of the report month e.g `SO_202308_ML1.shp`. Example path: `.../Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/Monitoring/Year_Month_template/IPC_ML1`. :::{Warning} Make sure to __not__ use the ML1_IDP data which comes in the .zip folder as well! :::

source.ph: name: Content of .zip file downloaded containing ML1 and ML2 IPC projections

target.ph: name: Content of .zip file downloaded containing ML1 and ML2 IPC projections

trans-unit.source: :::{tip} On the [main FEWSNET page](https://fews.net/), you can also sign up for information on latest updates via email. For this option scroll down to the end of the page and click on `Sign up for Emails`. You will get the option to choose updates only for Somalia.
trans-unit.target: :::{tip} On the [main FEWSNET page](https://fews.net/), you can also sign up for information on latest updates via email. For this option scroll down to the end of the page and click on `Sign up for Emails`. You will get the option to choose updates only for Somalia.

trans-unit.source: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS
trans-unit.target: Step 3: Loading data into QGIS

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into a QGIS-project so we can analyse the data.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, all the data needed will be loaded into a QGIS-project so we can analyse the data.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ No specific tools are needed, only QGIS.

trans-unit.source: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.
trans-unit.target: Open QGIS and create a [new project](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.html#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`.

trans-unit.source: Once the project is created, save the project in the folder you created in Step 1 (e.g. 2022_05). To do that, click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Give the project the same name as the folder you created (e.g. 2022_05). Then click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Once the project is created, save the project in the folder you created in Step 1 (e.g. 2022_05). To do that, click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Give the project the same name as the folder you created (e.g. 2022_05). Then click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Load all input data in [QGIS via drag and drop](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop). Click on `Project` → `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Load all input data in [QGIS via drag and drop](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop). Click on `Project` → `Save`.

trans-unit.source: From the folder you created in step 1
trans-unit.target: From the folder you created in step 1

trans-unit.source: ML1
trans-unit.target: ML1

trans-unit.source: From the `Fixes_data` folder:
trans-unit.target: From the `Fixes_data` folder:

trans-unit.source: district_pop_som
trans-unit.target: district_pop_som

trans-unit.source: WorldPop_som.tif
trans-unit.target: WorldPop_som.tif

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ QGIS project with all necessary data ready to be analysed.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ QGIS project with all necessary data ready to be analysed.

trans-unit.source: Step 4: Intersection of ML 1 data with the district polygons
trans-unit.target: Step 4: Intersection of ML 1 data with the district polygons

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The goal is to receive polygon layers which share both the borders and the attributes of both input layers.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The goal is to receive polygon layers which share both the borders and the attributes of both input layers.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Intersection`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#intersection)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Intersection`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_geoprocessing_wiki.html#intersection)

trans-unit.source: Instruction
trans-unit.target: Instruction

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Intersection window
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Intersection window

trans-unit.source: Click on `Vector` → `Geoprocessing Tools` → `Intersection`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Vector` → `Geoprocessing Tools` → `Intersection`.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: ML 1
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: ML 1

trans-unit.source: `Overlay layer`: district_pop_sum
trans-unit.target: `Overlay layer`: district_pop_sum

trans-unit.source: Under `Intersection` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to your monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_Intersection" and click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Intersection` click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to your monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_Intersection" and click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ After doing this for ML1, you should have one polygon layer containing all columns of ML1 and district_pop_sum.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ After doing this for ML1, you should have one polygon layer containing all columns of ML1 and district_pop_sum.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Intersection of ML 1 data with the district polygons
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Intersection of ML 1 data with the district polygons
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 5: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon
trans-unit.target: Step 5: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Here we calculate the population in each polygon of the intersection layer from step 4.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Here we calculate the population in each polygon of the intersection layer from step 4.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics)

trans-unit.source: Instruction
trans-unit.target: Instruction

trans-unit.source: Screenshot of the Zonal Statistics window
trans-unit.target: Screenshot of the Zonal Statistics window

trans-unit.source: In the `Toolbox` → Search for [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics).
trans-unit.target: In the `Toolbox` → Search for [`Zonal Statistics`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html#zonal-statistics).

trans-unit.source: Tip: If the `Toolbox` is not open, click `Processing` → `Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: Tip: If the `Toolbox` is not open, click `Processing` → `Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: "ML1_Intersection"
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: "ML1_Intersection"

trans-unit.source: `Raster Layer`: "som_ppp_2020_UNadj_constrained.tif"
trans-unit.target: `Raster Layer`: "som_ppp_2020_UNadj_constrained.tif"

trans-unit.source: `Statistics to calculate`: Only `Sum`
trans-unit.target: `Statistics to calculate`: Only `Sum`

trans-unit.source: Under `Zonal Statistics`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to you monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_zonal_statistic" and click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Zonal Statistics`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to you monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_zonal_statistic" and click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ The result should be the "ML1_zonal_statistic" as a polygon layer. This layer should have the same columns in the attribute table like ML1_Intersection __plus__ the column "_sum", which is the number of people living in the single parts of the polygons.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ The result should be the "ML1_zonal_statistic" as a polygon layer. This layer should have the same columns in the attribute table like ML1_Intersection __plus__ the column "_sum", which is the number of people living in the single parts of the polygons.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculation of Population per Intersection Polygon
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 6: Weighting of the Population based on IPC-Phase
trans-unit.target: Step 6: Weighting of the Population based on IPC-Phase

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is the weighting of the population in the five IPC phases as described in [IPC Data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index).
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is the weighting of the population in the five IPC phases as described in [IPC Data](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.html#ipc-population-weighted-index).

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} IPC-Population Weighted Index
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} IPC-Population Weighted Index

trans-unit.source: To better utilize the IPC data, a straightforward population-weighted index was created. This index assigns weights to relative population numbers based on their respective IPC classes, emphasizing the number of people in each IPC class rather than just the class itself. Additionally, populations in higher IPC classes are given more importance than those in lower classes. The index is calculated as follows:
trans-unit.target: To better utilize the IPC data, a straightforward population-weighted index was created. This index assigns weights to relative population numbers based on their respective IPC classes, emphasizing the number of people in each IPC class rather than just the class itself. Additionally, populations in higher IPC classes are given more importance than those in lower classes. The index is calculated as follows:

source.ph: `$ IPC\ Index = Weights \times \frac{District\ Pop\ per\ IPC\ Phase}{Total\ District\ Pop}$`

target.ph: `$ IPC\ Index = Weights \times \frac{District\ Pop\ per\ IPC\ Phase}{Total\ District\ Pop}$`

trans-unit.source: Where the weights are defined as:
trans-unit.target: Where the weights are defined as:

trans-unit.source: IPC Phase
trans-unit.target: IPC Phase

trans-unit.source: Weight
trans-unit.target: Weight

trans-unit.source: IPC 1
trans-unit.target: IPC 1

trans-unit.source: IPC 2
trans-unit.target: IPC 2

trans-unit.source: IPC 3
trans-unit.target: IPC 3

trans-unit.source: IPC 4
trans-unit.target: IPC 4

trans-unit.source: IPC 5
trans-unit.target: IPC 5

trans-unit.source: The IPC Index represents low-population districts equal to high-population districts. No underrepresentation of high food insecurity of small districts occurs. ::: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)
trans-unit.target: The IPC Index represents low-population districts equal to high-population districts. No underrepresentation of high food insecurity of small districts occurs. ::: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the layer “ML1_zonal_statistic” → `Open Attribute Table` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the layer “ML1_zonal_statistic” → `Open Attribute Table` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.

trans-unit.source: Check `Create new field`.
trans-unit.target: Check `Create new field`.

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column “pop_sum_weighted”
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column “pop_sum_weighted”

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)

trans-unit.source: Add the code block from Input into the `Expression` field.
trans-unit.target: Add the code block from Input into the `Expression` field.

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: When you are done, click ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits and switch off the editing mode by again clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) ([Video: Editing the attribute table](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md#attribute-table-data-editing)).
trans-unit.target: When you are done, click ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) to save your edits and switch off the editing mode by again clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png) ([Video: Editing the attribute table](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.md#attribute-table-data-editing)).

trans-unit.source: Step 7: Calculation of Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon
trans-unit.target: Step 7: Calculation of Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ In this step, we calculating the [IPC-Population Weighted Index](/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.md#ipc-population-weighted-index) for every small part of the polygon layer.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ In this step, we calculating the [IPC-Population Weighted Index](/content/GIS_AA/en_qgis_drought_trigger_somalia.md#ipc-population-weighted-index) for every small part of the polygon layer.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__[`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__[`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on "ML1_zonal_statistic" layer → `Attribute Table”` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on "ML1_zonal_statistic" layer → `Attribute Table”` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_attribute_table_wiki.html#attribute-table-data-editing) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.

trans-unit.source: Check `Create new field`.
trans-unit.target: Check `Create new field`.

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1"
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1"

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Decimal number (real)

trans-unit.source: Add the following code into the `Expression` field:
trans-unit.target: Add the following code into the `Expression` field:

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and turn off editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and turn off editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ The layer "ML1_zonal_statistic" should now have the column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1". The numbers in this column have to be smaller than in the "district" column.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ The layer "ML1_zonal_statistic" should now have the column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1". The numbers in this column have to be smaller than in the "district" column.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculation of Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculation of Population Proportion per Intersection Polygon
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 8: Calculate IPC Index per District
trans-unit.target: Step 8: Calculate IPC Index per District

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to calculate a population-weighted mean over the IPC classes per district. In this way, the amount of people living in a certain IPC class will be given more importance than just the area affected by a certain IPC class. The result is an IPC Index value for each district.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to calculate a population-weighted mean over the IPC classes per district. In this way, the amount of people living in a certain IPC class will be given more importance than just the area affected by a certain IPC class. The result is an IPC Index value for each district.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ `Join attribute by location (summary)`
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ `Join attribute by location (summary)`

trans-unit.source: Instruction
trans-unit.target: Instruction

trans-unit.source: Join attribute by location (summary)
trans-unit.target: Join attribute by location (summary)

trans-unit.source: In the `Toolbox` → Search for `Join attribute by location (summary)`.
trans-unit.target: In the `Toolbox` → Search for `Join attribute by location (summary)`.

trans-unit.source: Tip: If the `Toolbox` is not open, click `Processing` → `Toolbox`.
trans-unit.target: Tip: If the `Toolbox` is not open, click `Processing` → `Toolbox`.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer`: Select your "district_pop_som" layer.
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer`: Select your "district_pop_som" layer.

trans-unit.source: `Input Layer 2`: Select "ML1_zonal_statistic".
trans-unit.target: `Input Layer 2`: Select "ML1_zonal_statistic".

trans-unit.source: `Geometric predicate`: Select "Intersection".
trans-unit.target: `Geometric predicate`: Select "Intersection".

trans-unit.source: `Field to summarise`: Select "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1".
trans-unit.target: `Field to summarise`: Select "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1".

trans-unit.source: `Summaries to calculate`: Only check the option "mean".
trans-unit.target: `Summaries to calculate`: Only check the option "mean".

trans-unit.source: Under `Join Layer`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to you monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_IPC_Index" and click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Under `Join Layer`, click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png) → `Save to File` and navigate to you monitoring folder [Year_Month]. Give the output the name "ML1_IPC_Index" and click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Click `Run`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Run`.

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ As a result, your layer "ML1_IPC_Index" should have the column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1_mean". Furthermore, the number of rows should be the exact number of districts in Somalia and the polygons should have the exact shape of the districts.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ As a result, your layer "ML1_IPC_Index" should have the column "Index_per_IPCPolygon_ML1_mean". Furthermore, the number of rows should be the exact number of districts in Somalia and the polygons should have the exact shape of the districts.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculate IPC Index per District
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Calculate IPC Index per District
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 9: Evaluate Trigger Activation
trans-unit.target: Step 9: Evaluate Trigger Activation

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to gain a quick overview of possible trigger activation without having to revise the actual data. Instead, we will have a binary column with trigger = yes or trigger=no values.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ The purpose of this step is to gain a quick overview of possible trigger activation without having to revise the actual data. Instead, we will have a binary column with trigger = yes or trigger=no values.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field)

trans-unit.source: Right-click on "ML1_IPC_Index" layer → `Attribute Table` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on "ML1_IPC_Index" layer → `Attribute Table` → click on [`Field Calculator`](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_table_functions_wiki.html#calculate-field) ![](/fig/mActionCalculateField.png) to open the field calculator.

trans-unit.source: Check `Create new field`.
trans-unit.target: Check `Create new field`.

trans-unit.source: `Output field name`: Name the new column "Trigger_activation".
trans-unit.target: `Output field name`: Name the new column "Trigger_activation".

trans-unit.source: `Result field type`: Text (string).
trans-unit.target: `Result field type`: Text (string).

trans-unit.source: Add the code below into the `Expression` field.
trans-unit.target: Add the code below into the `Expression` field.

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: Code
trans-unit.target: Code

trans-unit.source: Click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).
trans-unit.target: Save the new column by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionSaveEdits.png) in the attribute table and end the editing mode by clicking on ![](/fig/mActionToggleEditing.png).

trans-unit.source: __Result:__ A layer with all districts of Somalia with a column of "Yes" and "No" values indicating whether the trigger levels have been reached or not.
trans-unit.target: __Result:__ A layer with all districts of Somalia with a column of "Yes" and "No" values indicating whether the trigger levels have been reached or not.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Evaluate Trigger Activation
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Evaluate Trigger Activation
:::

trans-unit.source: Step 10.: Visualising the Results
trans-unit.target: Step 10.: Visualising the Results

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Definition of how features are represented visually on the map.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Definition of how features are represented visually on the map.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [Symbology](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_styling_vector_data.html#symbology-for-vector-data)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [Symbology](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_styling_vector_data.html#symbology-for-vector-data)

trans-unit.source: __Trigger Activation__
trans-unit.target: __Trigger Activation__

trans-unit.source: Right-click on the "ML1_IPC_Index" layer → `Properties` → `Symbology`.
trans-unit.target: Right-click on the "ML1_IPC_Index" layer → `Properties` → `Symbology`.

trans-unit.source: In the down left corner click on `Style` → `Load Style`.
trans-unit.target: In the down left corner click on `Style` → `Load Style`.

trans-unit.source: In the new window click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png). Navigate to the “FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/layer_styles” folder and select the file __"Style_Trigger_Activation_ex.qml"__.
trans-unit.target: In the new window click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png). Navigate to the “FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/layer_styles” folder and select the file __"Style_Trigger_Activation_ex.qml"__.

trans-unit.source: Click `Open` → `Load Style`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Open` → `Load Style`.

trans-unit.source: Back in the "Layer Properties"-window, click `Apply` and `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Back in the "Layer Properties"-window, click `Apply` and `OK`.

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Info: Trigger Activation Layer :open: You will now see districts where no trigger is activated in green and districts with trigger activation in pink.
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Info: Trigger Activation Layer :open: You will now see districts where no trigger is activated in green and districts with trigger activation in pink.

trans-unit.source: The "Style_Trigger_Activation.qml" style layer is configured to show the district names only where the trigger is actually activated. If there is no trigger activation you can activate the admin 1 boundary layer for better map orientation (see __Administrative 2 Boundaries__ below). ::::
trans-unit.target: The "Style_Trigger_Activation.qml" style layer is configured to show the district names only where the trigger is actually activated. If there is no trigger activation you can activate the admin 1 boundary layer for better map orientation (see __Administrative 2 Boundaries__ below). ::::

trans-unit.source: __Administrative 2 Boundaries (Regions)__
trans-unit.target: __Administrative 2 Boundaries (Regions)__

trans-unit.source: Right click on the "Som_admin1_regions_UNDP.gqkp" (Regions) layer → `Properties` → `Symbology`.
trans-unit.target: Right click on the "Som_admin1_regions_UNDP.gqkp" (Regions) layer → `Properties` → `Symbology`.

trans-unit.source: In the down left corner click on `Style` → `Load Style`.
trans-unit.target: In the down left corner click on `Style` → `Load Style`.

trans-unit.source: In the new window click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png). Navigate to the “FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/layer_styles” folder and select the file __“SOM_regions_style_ex.qml”__.
trans-unit.target: In the new window click on the three points ![](/fig/Three_points.png). Navigate to the “FbF_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger/layer_styles” folder and select the file __“SOM_regions_style_ex.qml”__.

trans-unit.source: Click `Open` → `Load Style`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Open` → `Load Style`.

trans-unit.source: Back in the “Layer Properties” Window click `Apply` and `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Back in the “Layer Properties” Window click `Apply` and `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Add a the OpenStreetMap basemap by clicking on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add XYZ layer...` → Select the OpenStreetMap. Click `Add`. ([Wiki basemap](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.md)).
trans-unit.target: Add a the OpenStreetMap basemap by clicking on `Layer` → `Add Layer` → `Add XYZ layer...` → Select the OpenStreetMap. Click `Add`. ([Wiki basemap](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_basemaps_wiki.md)).

trans-unit.source: Place the OpenStreetMap basemap on the bottom.
trans-unit.target: Place the OpenStreetMap basemap on the bottom.

trans-unit.source: Delet all layers __exept__:
trans-unit.target: Delet all layers __exept__:

trans-unit.source: Trigger_activation
trans-unit.target: Trigger_activation

trans-unit.source: Som_admin1_regions_UNDP
trans-unit.target: Som_admin1_regions_UNDP

trans-unit.source: OpenStreetMap
trans-unit.target: OpenStreetMap

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Visualisation of results
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Visualisation of results
:::

trans-unit.source: Intervention Map __without__ Trigger activation
trans-unit.target: Intervention Map __without__ Trigger activation

trans-unit.source: Intervention Map __with__ Trigger activation
trans-unit.target: Intervention Map __with__ Trigger activation

trans-unit.source: Step 11: Making print map
trans-unit.target: Step 11: Making print map

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Visualisation of the map features in a printable map layout.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Visualisation of the map features in a printable map layout.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html)

trans-unit.source: If you have not done this before, delete all layers expect __Trigger_activation__, __Som_admin1_regions_UNDP__ and __OpenStreetMap__.
trans-unit.target: If you have not done this before, delete all layers expect __Trigger_activation__, __Som_admin1_regions_UNDP__ and __OpenStreetMap__.

trans-unit.source: Open a new print layout by clicking on `Project` → `New Print Layout` → enter the name of your current Project e.g "2024_01".
trans-unit.target: Open a new print layout by clicking on `Project` → `New Print Layout` → enter the name of your current Project e.g "2024_01".

trans-unit.source: Go the the `Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger` folder and drag and drop the file `Trigger_activation_Intervention_map_ex.qpt` in the print layout.
trans-unit.target: Go the the `Module_5_Exercise2_Drought_Monitoring_Trigger` folder and drag and drop the file `Trigger_activation_Intervention_map_ex.qpt` in the print layout.

trans-unit.source: Change the date to the current date by clicking on "Further map info…" in the items panel. Click on the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down. Here you can change the date in the `Main Properties` field.
trans-unit.target: Change the date to the current date by clicking on "Further map info…" in the items panel. Click on the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down. Here you can change the date in the `Main Properties` field.

trans-unit.source: If necessary, adjust the legend by clicking on the legend in the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down until you see the `Legend items` field. If it is not there check if you have to open the dropdown. Make sure `Auto update` is not checked.
trans-unit.target: If necessary, adjust the legend by clicking on the legend in the `Item Properties` tab and scroll down until you see the `Legend items` field. If it is not there check if you have to open the dropdown. Make sure `Auto update` is not checked.

trans-unit.source: Remove all items in the legend be clicking on the item and then on the red minus icon below.
trans-unit.target: Remove all items in the legend be clicking on the item and then on the red minus icon below.

trans-unit.source: Add __Trigger_activation__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Add __Trigger_activation__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `OK`.

trans-unit.source: Add __Som_admin1_regions_UNDP__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `OK`.
trans-unit.target: Add __Som_admin1_regions_UNDP__ to the legend by clicking on the green plus and click on the layer and click `OK`.

trans-unit.source:
:::{dropdown} Video: Making a print map
:::
trans-unit.target:
:::{dropdown} Video: Making a print map
:::

trans-unit.source: You can also adapt the template to your needs and preferences. See [how to use templates in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html).
trans-unit.target: You can also adapt the template to your needs and preferences. See [how to use templates in QGIS](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html).

trans-unit.source: Step 13.: Exporting Map
trans-unit.target: Step 13.: Exporting Map

trans-unit.source: __Purpose:__ Export the designed and finalised map layout in order to print it as a pdf or format of your choice.
trans-unit.target: __Purpose:__ Export the designed and finalised map layout in order to print it as a pdf or format of your choice.

trans-unit.source: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html)
trans-unit.target: __Tool:__ [Print Layout](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Module_4/en_qgis_map_design_2.html)

trans-unit.source: When you have finished the design of you map, you can export it as pdf or image file in different data types.
trans-unit.target: When you have finished the design of you map, you can export it as pdf or image file in different data types.

trans-unit.source: __Export as Image__
trans-unit.target: __Export as Image__

trans-unit.source: In the print layout click on `Layer` → `Export as Image`.
trans-unit.target: In the print layout click on `Layer` → `Export as Image`.

trans-unit.source: Chose the __Result__ folder in the folder you have created in step 1. Give the file the name of the project e.g 2022_04.
trans-unit.target: Chose the __Result__ folder in the folder you have created in step 1. Give the file the name of the project e.g 2022_04.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Save`.

trans-unit.source: The window "Image Export Options" will appear. Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: The window "Image Export Options" will appear. Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Now the image can be found in the result folder in the folder you created in Step 1.
trans-unit.target: Now the image can be found in the result folder in the folder you created in Step 1.

trans-unit.source: __Export as PDF__
trans-unit.target: __Export as PDF__

trans-unit.source: In the print layout click on `Layer` → `Export as PDF`.
trans-unit.target: In the print layout click on `Layer` → `Export as PDF`.

trans-unit.source: Chose the __Result__ folder in the folder you have created in step 1. Give the file the name of the project e.g 2022_04.
trans-unit.target: Chose the __Result__ folder in the folder you have created in step 1. Give the file the name of the project e.g 2022_04.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Save`.

trans-unit.source: The window "PDF Export Options" will appear. For the best results, select the `lossless` image compression.
trans-unit.target: The window "PDF Export Options" will appear. For the best results, select the `lossless` image compression.

trans-unit.source: Click `Save`.
trans-unit.target: Click `Save`.

trans-unit.source: Now the image can be found in the result folder in the folder you created in Step 1.
trans-unit.target: Now the image can be found in the result folder in the folder you created in Step 1.
